evidences.title: Celta Vigo 2-4 Barcelona (Nov 9, 2025) Game Analysis - ESPN
evidences.content: Again the hosts hit back, this time in some style, with Iglesias' shot from the edge of the penalty area beating Wojciech Szczesny for pace. However, they could not hold on to parity until half-time with Yamal firing home at the far post after Rashford's cross was deflected into his path. Rashford and Dani Olmo both had shots blocked as Barcelona continued to pile on the pressure before Lewandowski completed his hat trick, his first for Barça in over a year, in the 73rd minute to take his club tally to nine for the season. The Poland international met Rashford's inswinging corner at the near post and glanced a header across goal and inside the far post. It was the Englishman's sixth assist of the season, a better return than Yamal or Real Madrid's Vinícius Júnior. evidences.content: # Celta Vigo 2-4 Barcelona: Goals and highlights - LaLiga 25/26 Celta Vigo vs Barcelona - LaLiga 25/26 Hello everyone and welcome to another MARCA in English live blog, this time covering the LaLiga clash between Celta Vigo and Barcelona at the Estadio de Balaídos. The Catalans enter the game sitting second in the table with 25 points, five behind leaders Real Madrid, but their performances have raised as many questions as their results have provided reassurance. While their attacking play remains sharp, defensive issues have persisted, with 13 goals conceded in 11 league matches, a concern underlined by a chaotic 3-3 draw with Club Brugge in the Champions League on Wednesday night. [...] ## When is Celta Vigo vs Barcelona? Kick-off for Celta Vigo vs Barcelona is 21:00 CEST on Sunday, November 9. Fans in the UK and Ireland can tune in at 20:00.??Fans in the USA, meanwhile, will find Celta Vigo vs Barcelona starting at 15:00 ET / 12:00 PT. ## Where to watch Celta Vigo vs Barcelona? Fans in the USA can catch Celta Vigo vs Barcelona live on ESPN+, ESPN Deportes, and fuboTV. In the UK, the match will be shown on LaLiga TV and Premier Sports 2. © Noviembre 2025 Unidad Editorial Infor...

evidences.title: Analysis Celta de Vigo vs FC Barcelona - Ligas y Partidos
evidences.content: From the outset, FC Barcelona showed its intention to control the game, reflected in a crushing 61% possession. This superiority translated into multiple opportunities, accumulating a total of 21 shots, 9 of which were on target. On the other hand, Celta tried to respond with its limited approaches, managing only 5 total shots. There is no detailed information about the goal scorers or the specific minutes in which they occurred, but clearly, Barcelona's efficiency was decisive in scoring four times against Celta's two. However, statistics show that despite having fewer opportunities, Celta managed to score twice in the few chances they had. [...] On a disciplinary level, the match was intense. Celta de Vigo received one yellow card while FC Barcelona had a more aggressive behavior with five yellow cards and one red. This could have influenced the dynamics of the game, especially defensively for the visiting team. Regarding corners, Barcelona generated more opportunities from corners with a total of five compared to Celta's two. This dominance in set pieces may have contributed to the final result. evidences.title: Celta Vigo handed late boost for Barcelona clash - Yahoo Sports
evidences.content: As per a report from Mundo Deportivo, Radu has been given the medical green light to feature from the off against Barcelona on Sunday. The 28-year-old has played a leading role in Vigo across the season to date, racking up 11 La Liga starts. Radu will now be hopeful of playing his part in keeping alive a stellar recent run of form for Claudio Giráldez’s troops, in the midst of a five-game win streak across all competitions. This comes amid confirmation that goalkeeper Ionuț Radu is ready to feature in the club’s meeting with Barcelona. Shot-stopper Radu was for his part forced to sit out in midweek, as Celta secured an impressive victory at Dinamo Zagreb in the Europa League. This came owing to an injury concern in his right hand.
